title: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhead: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

Lior Amihai, director of Israeli NGO Peace Now: 'The coalition in power wants to annex the territories in the West Bank and establish a racist state based on Jewish supremacy'
subhead: Driven by a messianic ideology, Benjamin Netanyahu's government has bureaucratized the expansion of West Bank settlements since late 2022. But according to the director of a major peace advocacy group, the majority of Israeli public opinion does not support this policy.

title: Climate change suspected of contributing to recent surge in hantavirus cases in Argentina
subhead: During the 2025-2026 summer season, 106 cases of hantavirus infection were reported in Argentina, nearly double the previous year, and at much higher levels than in the past.

title: Djokovic knocked out in French Open third round by Fonseca
subhead: João Fonseca defeated Novak Djokovic 4-6, 4-6, 6-3, 7-5, 7-5 in the third round of the French Open on Friday. 'I was trying to hit the ball as fast as I could. Djokovic, he does not miss,' Fonseca said. As for Djokovic, he said his opponent 'deserved to win,' and when asked if he would be back next year, he twice replied: 'I don't know.'

title: Champions League final in Budapest highlights Orban's contested legacy
subhead: The decision to award the final of Europe's most prestigious competition to Hungary is a result of the sports-diplomacy strategy pursued by former Hungarian prime minister Viktor Orban. This approach has been sharply criticized by his successor, Péter Magyar.

38° at 11 am. French students and staff struggle with unbearable classroom temperatures
subhead: Across France, primary, middle and high schools have reported the severity of the situation in their overheated classrooms. Education professionals are criticizing the lack of response in recent years from both local authorities, who are responsible for school buildings, and from the Education Ministry, in charge of the safety of students and staff.

title: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
subhead: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent

title: How China uses influencers to polish its image
subhead: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.
